title: Arps Decline Curve Analysis
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Arps Decline Curve Analysis: Arps decline curve analysis: fit exponential/hyperbolic/harmonic decline to production history to forecast EUR. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.arps.hyperbolic_decline…); recovery is posed as a parameter_estimation problem. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~10; rate_interference_from_offset_wells, compression_uplift_events set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: q(t) = q_i / (1 + b*D_i*t)^{1/b} (hyperbolic); b=0 exponential, b=1 harmonic
E.world_state_x: decline parameters (q_i, D_i, b) and EUR (estimated ultimate recovery)
E.observation_y: monthly production rate time series {q(t_k)} for k=1..N_months

W.regime: Existence of the recovered decline_parameter_vector is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 10); rate_interference_from_offset_wells dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Lognormal sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: EUR uncertainty ±20% for conventional wells with 2+ years history. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~10. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |rate_interference_from_offset_wells|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_months * N_params) for nonlinear regression per iteration
